Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:38 ET
NVDA straddle surge ahead of earnings masks broader dispersion play setup
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive NVDA straddle positioning (270K+ contracts) dominates flow ahead of Tuesday earnings, obscuring cleaner signals elsewhere
  • 2. Index puts heavy (1.79 P/C) versus single-name neutral creates textbook dispersion opportunity with 0.04 implied correlation
  • 3. Positive gamma environment ($+3.2B SPY GEX) should dampen volatility into critical GDP/FOMC week starting Thursday
NARRATIVE — positive gamma cushion

NVDA's pre-earnings volatility circus is drowning out more nuanced positioning signals, with 270K+ contracts split between 140K Apr 22 200P and 131K Apr 22 202.5C/205C creating a massive $200-205 straddle ahead of Tuesday's print. This earnings-driven noise masks the more interesting structural trade developing across the broader market, where index P/C at 1.79 versus single-name P/C at 1.05 signals institutional dispersion hedging into a low 0.04 implied correlation environment. The vol surface reveals meaningful divergences that support this dispersion thesis, with 5 names showing cheap vol/rich skew characteristics (IBIT, EWZ, ORCL, ADBE, MU) and another 5 displaying vol high/skew flat patterns (KWEB, ABBV, WMT, QCOM, BKNG). These cross-signals suggest single-name volatility is more efficiently priced than index vol, creating the classic setup for long single-name/short index dispersion trades that institutional desks typically exploit. Dealer positioning at $+3.2B SPY GEX provides a stabilizing backdrop heading into a catalyst-heavy week, with GDP Q1 Advance Thursday and Personal Income data next Monday preceding what could be a volatile FOMC setup. The combination of positive gamma dampening and elevated put positioning (SPY 95K Apr 24 701P, 94K Apr 22 705P) suggests institutions are positioned defensively but expecting contained ranges — until the macro catalysts hit.

Layer 1: Sentiment +21Layer 2: GEX +$3.2BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.79/1.05Layer 4: IC 0.04 dispersion

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
